note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of. FILED data (10-K/10-Q line items) rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. Price sits -18.7% off 52wk high / +29.7% above 52wk low -- squarely in the middle of the range, well outside the 3% low/high-hug band on both ends, so this is NOT a hug situation (T2 should pass cleanly on distance alone).

live_fields.value: FY2026 guidance (raised at Q1 2026 report, ~2026-04-23): revenue $47.3B-$48.1B (up from $46.3B-$47.2B), representing +6% to +8% reported growth (+3-4% organic); adjusted EPS $24.64-$25.12 (up from $24.22-$24.80). Clario acquisition adds ~$900M revenue and $0.32 adjusted EPS (net of financing costs) to the guide. CONFIRMED still the latest authoritative guidance as of as_of 2026-07-05: Q2 2026 earnings (the next scheduled guidance-update event) is confirmed via a 2026-07-01 BusinessWire notice to occur 2026-07-23 -- i.e., AFTER this dossier's as_of date, so no newer guidance revision exists to find. Also confirmed: Q2 2026 quarterly dividend of $0.47/share declared, payable 2026-07-15 (record date 2026-06-15) -- consistent with the 10% dividend increase disclosed in Q1 2026 results (facts.md E32), no independent guidance-relevant signal beyond confirming continued capital-return cadence.

TMO does NOT disclose China as a standalone geographic segment (unlike GEHC) -- only 'Asia-Pacific' (~$8.10B FY2025 / ~$7.96B or 18.55% of total FY2024). Management commentary describes full-year 2025 China organic revenue as declining 'mid-single digits', a deceleration from FY2024's 'low-single-digit' Asia-Pacific growth. Q1 2026: Analytical Instruments organic revenue -2%, explicitly attributed to academic/government demand softness in BOTH the US and China. No source found this run quantifies a precise China-only revenue percentage or identifies a specific Chinese competitor gaining disclosed share in analytical instruments -- this is the single largest disclosure/evidence gap in this dossier relative to the GEHC exemplar (which had an explicit, quantified China revenue line) and is flagged as the top research priority for the next refresh.

BIOSECURE Act signed into law December 2025 -- restricts US-government-linked biopharma customers from contracting with designated Chinese CRO/CDMO 'companies of concern' (WuXi AppTec, WuXi Biologics named in general industry coverage, not a TMO-specific primary disclosure). TMO's PPD clinical-research/CDMO business (within Laboratory Products and Biopharma Services, 53.8% of FY2025 revenue) is positioned as a primary beneficiary; the $8.875B Clario acquisition (closed 2026-03-24) directly expands this capability, with its ~$900M FY2026 revenue contribution already embedded in raised guidance. Separately, tariff cost impact is estimated (via search-derived commentary, NOT independently confirmed against a primary TMO disclosure this run) at ~$375M -- exact period basis ambiguous, flagged OPEN.

Real, active AI-enabled product pipeline as of ASMS 2026 (annual mass spectrometry conference, ~2026-05/06): Orbitrap Tribrid Apex and Orbitrap Excedion mass spectrometers paired with AI-enabled software (Proteoform Studio, Proteome Discoverer 3.4, BioPharma Finder 5.5); recent MSAID and Proteinaceous acquisitions feed AI capability into the platform; NVIDIA strategic collaboration announced 2026-01-12 for AI-based lab automation at scale. HOWEVER: zero sources found this run quantify AI-attributable revenue, ASP premium, or attach rate anywhere in the business -- exactly parallel to the GEHC exemplar's equivalent gap (O7).
